nuggets.headline: Kendrick Lamar's longtime engineer Derek Ali uses parallel processing on his vocals
nuggets.text: The punch and clarity of Kendrick's vocals that cut through the mix without sounding squashed come from his go-to mixing engineer, Derek "MixedByAli" Ali. Ali often creates a heavily compressed duplicate of the vocal track and blends it back in with the original, adding power and presence while preserving the main take's dynamics.

source.title: Derek Ali Mixing: Secrets Of A Hip-Hop Sound Engineer
source.quoteSnippet: One of Ali’s signature techniques is his use of parallel processing. This involves creating multiple copies of a signal and processing each one differently before blending them back together. For example, he might create a heavily compressed version of a vocal track and then blend it with the original, unprocessed version to add punch and clarity without sacrificing dynamic range.

nuggets.imageCaption: Engineer Derek "MixedByAli" Ali is a key architect of Kendrick Lamar's sound.

nuggets.headline: "HUMBLE." was the first song recorded for DAMN. and was almost given away
nuggets.text: This track set the tone for the entire album, as it was the very first one recorded for the project. After the session, an agreement was made to release it on producer Mike Will Made It's own album, "Ransom 2." It was only after others intervened that Kendrick was convinced to keep the track for himself, securing the lead single for "DAMN."

source.title: Humble (song)
source.quoteSnippet: "Humble" was the first song to be recorded for Damn. After recording, it was initially agreed upon that it would be released on Mike Will Made's debut album Ransom 2, but others convinced Lamar to keep it for his upcoming album.

nuggets.imageCaption: A still from the iconic music video for "HUMBLE."

nuggets.headline: For more from Kendrick Lamar's inner circle, listen to Jay Rock
nuggets.text: You already know Kendrick's crew, but it's time to dig into the work of his Black Hippy partner, Jay Rock. Both artists came up on the Top Dawg Entertainment label, sharing an apocalyptic vision of Los Angeles and often working with the same in-house producers like Sounwave. For a masterclass in modern West Coast gangster rap that shares DNA with "DAMN.", start with Jay Rock's album "Redemption."

source.title: Does kendrick lamar produce his own music? - California Learning Resource Network
source.quoteSnippet: The sonic landscape of Kendrick Lamar’s music is largely shaped by his collaborations with a core group of producers... Sounwave: Longtime collaborator, instrumental in shaping Kendrick’s signature sound.

artistSummary: Kendrick Lamar operates as the executive producer of his own sonic universe, meticulously selecting a village of collaborators to execute his vision. His projects are defined by a hands-on approach, shaping everything from the overarching concept down to the final mix.
